head.hi: The Ambassador in Japan (Forbes) to the Secretary of State

div.p:
80. I have conferred with the British and Italian Ambassadors and communicated with the French Ambassador in regard to your telegram No. 62;February 24, 1932, 6 p.m., Foreign Relations, Japan, 1931–1941, vol. i, p. 200. and they, under their instructions, do not feel either a joint visit or identic note feasible. They are calling individually on the Minister. I have accordingly left a memorandum with the Foreign Minister in writing in accordance with your instructions, as near as we could make it word for word a copy of which will be sent by mail. The Foreign Minister received me and stated that the representations of the United States would receive his most careful consideration and that
he would refer the matter immediately to the proper department and let us hear from him later.
